key_quote: The supported fragment severely limits practical applicability: no pointers, heap, structs, strings, floating point, or concurrency — exactly the features that make C-to-Rust translation difficult.
rationale: The σ-bridge concept is acknowledged as novel and well-motivated by all reviewers. The problems are all about insufficient depth: the fragment is too narrow (0% on Memory/String categories), CEGAR convergence is too low (16.7%), parsers are unvalidated, and comparison with c2rust is missing. Expanding the supported fragment would transform this from proof-of-concept to practical tool.
